name: AWS CodeArtifact
cost: Pay-as-you-go, no upfront fees or commitments. Always-free tier (not time-limited): 2 GB storage + 100,000 requests/month. Beyond that (US regions): storage $0.05/GB-month; requests $0.05 per 10,000 (downloads, uploads, API calls, and each asset ingested from a public repo all count). Data transfer in from the internet is free; data transfer out is billed at standard AWS rates; same-region transfer to other AWS services is free.
language: Undisclosed (proprietary AWS managed service; implementation language not published)
last_updated: 2026 (actively maintained AWS managed service; user-guide docs last published June 2026; continuous release cadence, e.g. package groups and Cargo/Ruby/Swift formats added in recent years)
perf_notes: No public throughput/latency benchmarks (managed service). Assets stored in AWS-managed Amazon S3, encrypted with a per-domain KMS key, deduplicated across repositories in a domain. Per-account request-rate throttling and service quotas apply; quota increases available on request.
scale_notes: Fully managed and highly available: artifacts replicated across multiple Availability Zones within a region, elastic scaling handled by AWS (no nodes to operate). Organized as domains containing repositories with upstream/external-connection chains; cross-account domain sharing via resource policies; private network access via VPC endpoints (PrivateLink). Default service quotas (repos per domain, asset sizes, package versions) with limit-increase requests.
summary: AWS's fully managed, pay-as-you-go artifact repository for hosting private packages and proxying public ones across multiple ecosystems (PyPI, npm, Maven, NuGet, Cargo, Ruby, Swift, generic), authenticated entirely through AWS IAM/STS with built-in dependency-confusion protection.
extras: Multi-ecosystem: PyPI, npm, Maven/Gradle, NuGet, Ruby (gems), Cargo (crates), Swift, and generic packages in one service

Python compatibility is deliberately narrow: CodeArtifact supports the Legacy /simple/<project>/ endpoint (so pip install/download work) but NOT the /simple/ root index, and supports neither PyPI's XML-RPC nor JSON APIs (no PEP 691/700 JSON simple)
Domains group repositories sharing a single KMS encryption key and deduplicating asset storage across repos
Upstream repositories + external connections give pull-through caching of public registries with on-demand (lazy) ingestion and retention
Package origin controls (publish/upstream Allow/Block) defend against dependency-substitution/confusion attacks; configurable per-package or in bulk via package groups
Package groups apply origin-control and permission policies to many packages at once via naming patterns

Keyless CI publishing via AWS IAM OIDC federation (e.g. GitHub Actions OIDC -> STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity -> 12h CodeArtifact auth token) -- not PyPI-style Trusted Publishing, the index does not accept OIDC tokens directly
All API activity logged in AWS CloudTrail (structured JSON); EventBridge events fire on package-version state changes
Cross-account domain sharing and VPC endpoint (PrivateLink) private access
Infrastructure-as-code support via CloudFormation, CDK, and Terraform; managed through AWS Console, CLI, and SDKs
Package version status lifecycle: Published / Unlisted / Archived / Disposed (delete-package-versions and dispose-package-versions APIs)
Vulnerability scanning is not native -- achieved by integrating Amazon Inspector Code Security / SCA against the repository
